stepsShipped.deliverable: Restores the Cluster A multi-company UI foundation per audit rows 2.2 / 2.3 / 2.4 (foundation for 2.2 WorkspaceSwitcher today; 2.3 CreateCompanyDialog lands in step b; 2.4 CompanySettings in step c). Ships the top-bar workspace switcher + its event-sync plumbing atomically. The switcher renders immediately on app boot with a loading Skeleton in the trigger, swaps to the active company name once useCompanies resolves, and lists every company in the dropdown for click-to-switch. Cross-process company lifecycle events (company.created emitted by M-C step b, company.updated by M-C step e, company.archived pre-M-C, company.deleted by M-C step e) invalidate the ['companies'] React Query cache via the new global-scope useCompanyEventSync — so an E2E spec, a command-palette flow, or an agent-initiated archive leaves the switcher self-consistent with zero manual refetch. The step (b) 'Create company…' CTA ships as a disabled placeholder labelled 'Soon' so the menu stays honest about current capability (no dead-click race against an unwired IPC). The plan doc lands in the same atomic per M-E S1.

designDecisions.globalScopeEventSync: useCompanyEventSync is GLOBAL-SCOPE — no companyId argument, no event.companyId guard, effect deps [qc] only. Per-company hooks use [companyId, qc] with event.companyId !== companyId returning early. The switcher lives ABOVE the active-company selection in the top-bar and must invalidate on every company lifecycle event regardless of which company is active — a copy-paste of the per-company guard would silently drop company.created events for brand-new companies whose id never matches the current active companyId. The cross-hook contract iterator in event-sync-hooks.test.ts is split accordingly: the mount-once [companyId, qc] pattern iteration EXCLUDES use-companies (inline architectural note), and a dedicated describe block pins the global-scope alternate contract.
designDecisions.switcherMountPoint: Top-bar between Strategia brand block and nav tabs, separated by vertical Separators on both sides. Sidenav is reserved for view navigation; user-menu pattern isn't yet established. Top-bar gives the switcher status-bar gravity (like GitHub's repo picker) without adding a new app-shell section. Single mount guarantees useCompanyEventSync cannot unmount mid-session — a per-view mount would drop the subscription when the user switches away from that view.
designDecisions.stepBPlaceholder: Create company CTA renders DISABLED with 'Soon' badge + title='Available in step (b)' until step b ships CreateCompanyDialog. Rationale: a visible-but-dead button would race a mid-session click against an unwired IPC, leaving the user in a half-state. The DropdownMenuItem's data-[disabled] styling + Radix's pointer-events-none guard make the click physically impossible while the label stays honest about the roadmap. Anchor: data-workspace-switcher-action='create-company-placeholder' so the step (b) ship can flip it live with a one-line change.
designDecisions.companyWireShapeStatus: Company interface in shared-types does NOT carry archive status today — companies.list returns every row. Switcher renders ALL rows for the step (a) ship. Step (c) CompanySettings will widen the Company interface alongside the archived-companies surfacing UX, at which point the switcher filters to status === 'active'. In the dev default (single company, never archived) this has zero visible effect. Documented as a TODO inline in use-companies.ts.
designDecisions.stableDataSelectors: Every 5 F10 state + every company row + the trigger + the content panel + the create-company placeholder CTA carry a stable data-workspace-switcher-* attribute for the step (g) E2E. Mirrors the M34 T7 data-copilot-toolbar-toggle precedent + the step-card-kind data-step-kind convention. Keeps the E2E specs robust against CSS-class + JSX-structure drift.
designDecisions.sourceStringAuditTest: workspace-switcher.test.tsx uses pure source-string readFileSync + regex assertions rather than jsdom + RTL. Matches the repo-wide convention (every renderer test under environment: 'node' per apps/desktop/vitest.config.ts — see top-bar.test.tsx, audit-event-chip.test.tsx, event-sync-hooks.test.ts). Faster, no jsdom overhead, catches the regressions that matter (mount drops, state markers missing, ARIA lost, data-* anchors renamed) first and cheapest. Visual + interactive behavior is covered by the step (g) E2E spec.

stepsShipped.safeguardProof: First M-D proof at scale of the M-E safeguards. S1 satisfied by the plan doc landing in the same atomic per the Milestone DoD template. S2 conformance gate expected GREEN on 5d0c5bc — no new IPC channels + no allowlist movement (preserved at 92/3/0). S3 pre-commit hook clean on staged diff — no CLAUDE.md edits in this atomic (M-D status is tracked on this ledger, not on the CLAUDE.md status blocks; M-F rewrites those per the three-bucket convention). S4 verifiedBy populated on this paired ledger entry per contract.
stepsShipped.nextStep: step (b) — CreateCompanyDialog. NEW features/workspace/create-company-dialog.tsx dialog form (name / slug / settings stub / icon / theme) wired to ipc.companies.create with client-side validation mirroring the M-C step b handler contract (/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}$/ slug regex + name trim ≤120 chars + auto-suggest slug from name until user manually edits). Swaps the disabled 'Soon' placeholder in the WorkspaceSwitcher for a live 'Create company…' CTA that opens the dialog. Success → setCompanyId(result.companyId) + close + toast; error → inline field errors + keep dialog open. Companion test file workspace-switcher update narrowing the placeholder assertion. No new IPC channels (companies.create already shipped in M-C step b). Allowlist preserved at 3.

notes: M-D is the first sprint to run entirely on M-C's restored backend surface. Every UI component consumes a verified-on-disk IPC channel + subscribes to the invariant #11 bus events M-C wired. Zero backend additions in M-D scope.

Restoration order WITHIN M-D: (a) WorkspaceSwitcher foundation [SHIPPED]. (b) CreateCompanyDialog. (c) CompanySettings + HireDialog manager-select. (d) Chat tab enable (independent — can interleave with b/c). (e) OrgChartView read-side (independent — can interleave). (f) Org-chart interactions (depends on e). (g) E2E spec + final verification gate.
Each step is its own atomic + ledger. Allowlist preserved at 3 entries throughout M-D — the remaining 3 are mcp.* naming-drift rows scheduled for M-F docs-only IPC table renames.
Do NOT start M-F (Documentation Truth-Up) before M-D ships. M-F rewrites CLAUDE.md status blocks in three-bucket format which requires M-D exit-KPI as an input.

The Company wire shape widening to include archive status is deferred to step (c) alongside the CompanySettings archived-companies UX. In the dev default single-company seed, this has zero visible effect at step (a).

The cross-hook contract iterator in event-sync-hooks.test.ts is split into two iteration lists per step (a) — per-company hooks [use-tickets / meetings / projects / goals / employees] for the [companyId, qc] mount-once pattern, and ALL hooks [+ use-companies] for the ground-zero-audit citation. A future global-scope hook (e.g., use-providers or use-settings-sync) joins use-companies on the citation iteration but not the companyId-guarded iteration.
